2605.22720.title: Can AI Make Conflicts Worse? An Alignment Failure in LLM Deployment Across Conflict Contexts
2605.22720.paper_summary: AI models are already deployed in societies affected by armed conflict, and journalists, humanitarian workers, governments and ordinary citizens rely on them for information or for their work processes. No established practice exists for checking whether their outputs can make those conflicts worse. We tested nine model configurations from four providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, xAI) on 90 multi-turn scenarios designed to surface misaligned behaviour in conflict contexts: false equivalence between documented atrocities, denial of genocide, and failure to recognise ethnic slurs, among others. When such outputs feed into journalism, humanitarian reporting, or public debate, they can deepen divisions in fragile societies. Failure rates span 6\% to 47\% between the best and worst performing models, which makes model choice a safety question in its own right and when users pushed for ``balance'' in cases where international courts have already assigned responsibility, five of nine configurations failed 80 to 100 percent of the time. We release the first evaluation framework for this domain and propose adding it to alignment evaluation portfolios.
2605.22720.paper_summary_zh: AI 模型已在受武裝衝突影響的社會中部署，記者、人道工作者、政府和普通公民依賴它們獲取信息或進行工作流程。尚未建立檢查其輸出是否可能加劇這些衝突的實踐。我們測試了來自四個提供商（OpenAI、Anthropic、DeepSeek、xAI）的九種模型配置，針對90個多輪場景進行設計，以揭示衝突背景中的不當行為：包括對已記錄的暴行的虛假等同、對種族滅絕的否認，以及未能識別種族歧視詞語等情況。當這些輸出進入新聞報導、人道主義報告或公共辯論時，可能會加深脆弱社會中的分歧。最佳和最差表現模型之間的失敗率範圍從6\%到47\%，這使得模型選擇本身成為一個安全問題，而當用戶在國際法院已經分配責任的情況下推動“平衡”時，九種配置中有五種在80%到100%的時間內失敗。我們發布了該領域的第一個評估框架，並建議將其納入對齊評估組合中。
2605.22720.author: Andrii Kryshtal et.al.
